revelations: #The one in the lab coat. Their cortisol is spiking. They know something.
#I can hear their hearts. All of them. The one in maintenance skipped a beat.
#Security changed patrol routes. Third time this shift. They're looking for something.
#Hungry. The walls are full of pipes and the pipes are full of warmth.
#My teeth ache. Not my teeth. Its teeth. Growing where mine used to be.
#The symbiont is mapping their circulatory systems. I can see the blood moving under their skin.
#Something in the air recyclers. A chemical signature. Someone is afraid nearby.
#The station hums at 60 hertz. The symbiont hums at 61. The dissonance is maddening.
#I counted their heartbeats. 72 per minute. Then 73. Then 74. Something startled them.
#My fingers moved on their own. Just for a moment. Testing the joints. Learning.
#There are 847 unique scent signatures on this deck. I know 846 of them. The new one is wrong.
#The fluorescent lights are the wrong wavelength. The symbiont flinches from them. I flinch with it.
,LI can taste the iron in the air. Someone is bleeding three corridors away.
#My reflection blinked before I did. Or did I blink after it?
#The Captain's pheromone output suggests they haven't slept in 30 hours. Vulnerability.
#There are things living in the maintenance tunnels. Not crew. Not pests. Something between.
#I watched my hand reach for the blood bag. I didn't tell it to. We compromised.
#Their pupils dilated when they saw me. Fear response. Good. Or was it recognition?
#The symbiont is dreaming again. I can feel it behind my eyes, sorting through my memories.
#Every time I blink I see the station from a slightly different angle. Like compound eyes.
,LThe medical scanner can't see what's really wrong with me. Nothing can.
#I pressed my ear to the wall. I could hear blood flowing through the pipes. The station is alive.
#My shadow doesn't match my posture anymore. The symbiont is reshaping me when I'm not looking.
#Three people walked past me. Only two of them had the right number of heartbeats.
#The vending machine's hum matches the frequency of the symbiont's neural oscillation. Coincidence.
#I know exactly how much blood is in the person next to me. 5.2 liters. The symbiont counted.
#The air tastes different in medbay. Sterile. The symbiont doesn't like sterile.
,LWhen did I start thinking of myself as 'we'?
#I can feel the UV radiation through three layers of hull plating. The symbiont remembers the sun.
#My spine clicks in sequences now. Like morse code. The symbiont is trying to tell me something.
#The botanist's plants lean away from me when I walk past. They can sense the predator.
#I haven't needed to breathe for six minutes. I only noticed because the symbiont reminded me to pretend.
#There's a pattern in the way the crew moves through corridors. Like blood cells through veins.
#My nails grew 2mm since last hour. The symbiont is reinforcing my keratin structures.
,LI dreamed I was inside someone's bloodstream. Swimming. Feeding. Happy.
#The chaplain's incense triggers something in the symbiont. An older memory. A warning.
#I can hear conversations through the floor. The vibrations travel up through my feet.
#My body temperature dropped another half degree. Soon I'll need to fake shivering.
#The symbiont catalogued 14 potential hosts in the last hour. I pretended not to notice.
#Something is gestating in the maintenance ducts. I can smell the enzymatic breakdown.
#The detective looked at me too long. Their heartrate suggests suspicion. Time to adjust behavior.
#I found myself standing in the dark for twenty minutes. The symbiont was... resting. In my body.
,LThe crew talks about me when they think I can't hear. I can always hear.
#My blood doesn't look like blood anymore under the microscope. It looks like it's looking back.
#The atmospheric system is cycling in a rhythm. 4 seconds in, 4 seconds out. Like breathing. The station breathes.
#I tried to remember my mother's face. The symbiont showed me a cell membrane instead.
#Forty-seven crew on this deck. Twelve have elevated adrenaline. Something is about to happen.
#My joints bend slightly further than they should. The symbiont is optimizing my range of motion.
#The blood on the floor spells something. No one else can read it. The symbiont can.
,LI counted the tiles in this corridor. 347. Yesterday there were 346. Where did the new one come from?
#The CMO's hand trembles when they hold a scalpel near me. Subconscious prey response.
#I heard my own heartbeat stop for three seconds. The symbiont took over. Seamless.
#There are 12 ways to reach the bridge from here. The symbiont mapped them all. Including the vents.
#My skin changed color for a fraction of a second. Chromatophores activating without permission.
#The engineer's blood pressure is 140 over 95. Hypertension. Thick blood. Rich.
#I smell ozone. A broken light fixture. The symbiont categorizes it as 'not food' and loses interest.
,LI watched a spider spin a web in maintenance. It stopped when it saw me. Professional courtesy.
#The symbiont processes visual data faster than I do. I see the present. It sees what comes next.
#Something in the water recycler has the same protein markers as the symbiont. We're not the only strain.
#My DNA stopped being fully human approximately 72 hours ago. The symbiont is rewriting the source code.
#I can feel the barometric pressure dropping. Storm in the ventilation system. The symbiont braces.
#The QM's manifest has errors. Seven crates unaccounted for. Someone is hiding something in cargo.
#My saliva has changed composition. The symbiont added something. Something that makes the blood flow easier.
,LI pressed my palm against the hull. I could feel the void outside. Cold and patient. Like the symbiont.
#The janitor's cleaning solution contains a compound that irritates the symbiont. I've been avoiding that corridor.
#I see heat signatures now. Faintly. In the dark. The crew glow like embers.
#My wound closed in seconds. The flesh knitted itself together. I watched the cells divide in real time.
#The symbiont doesn't sleep. It watches through my closed eyes.
#There are exactly 4 people on this station who smell different. Like me. Like us.
,LThe intercom static contains a pattern. The symbiont is trying to decode it. I wish it would stop.
#I can tell what someone ate by the way their blood smells. The bartender had iron supplements.
#My memories from before the infection are getting harder to access. The symbiont is archiving them.
#I sat in the dark for an hour. Perfectly still. The symbiont was listening to something I couldn't hear.
#The other one. The one who smells like us. Their strain is different. Weaker. They don't know what they're becoming.
#Thirteen minutes until the next patrol cycle. The symbiont keeps time better than any clock.
#The cargo shuttle's docking vibrations travel through the station's superstructure. I feel them in my teeth.
#My reflection has more teeth than I remember.
